rule_summary: Freshman 0-23cr; Sophomore 24-59; Junior 60-89; Senior 90+
Courses with standing_requirement soft tag warn if student is below min standing; min_standing inferred from course level digit minus 1 when not explicit
Students placed on college academic probation per individual college policies
notes: Requires GPA input; college-specific thresholds vary

rule_summary: Students with 24 or fewer attempted credits dismissed if cumulative GPA below 1.500
notes: Checked at end of each spring term

rule_summary: Students with more than 24 attempted credits dismissed if cumulative GPA below 2.000
notes: Checked at end of each spring term

rule_summary: Full-time status requires minimum 12 credits per fall/spring term; 9-11 is three-quarter time; 6-8 is half-time
Normal full-time undergraduate load is 15-18 credits per fall/spring term
Arts and Sciences students max 20 credits per fall/spring term excluding J-session
notes: No CAS programs in planner yet

rule_summary: Business Administration students max 19 credits per fall/spring term excluding J-session; overload needs dean approval
notes: semester_recommender.py semester_warnings; currently applied universally since all programs are CoBA

rule_summary: Communication students max 19 credits per fall/spring term
Education students max 20 credits per fall/spring term
Engineering students max 20 credits per fall/spring term
Health Sciences students max 19 credits per fall/spring term
Nursing students max 18 credits per fall/spring term
Maximum 8 credits per summer session; 16 credits per summer term total
notes: semester_recommender.py warns if summer recs exceed 16 total credits

rule_summary: Maximum 4 credits per J-session
Juniors and seniors may exceed college max credit load with dean/dean designee consent
Non-degree students normally limited to maximum 7 credits per term
Students may not pursue credit at another institution while enrolled at MU unless authorized by dean
Minimum 120 credits earned required for baccalaureate degree
notes: Not all students use MarqBot for full graduation planning; most pick 1-3 semesters not all 8. Reference only for now.

rule_summary: Minimum 2.000 cumulative GPA required for graduation
Minimum 45 credits must be earned at Marquette; up to 15 of 45 may be from approved study abroad
Minimum 24 upper-division credits must be earned at Marquette including approved study abroad
At least 30 of the final 36 credits must be earned at Marquette or approved study abroad/contractual agreement
Minimum one-half of credits in the major must be earned at Marquette
Minimum one-half of credits in the minor must be earned at Marquette
A minimum of 15 credits in the major must be taken at Marquette
A minimum of 9 credits in the minor must be taken at Marquette
Students must clear all I IC IE and NG grades before graduation
Only degree-seeking students may declare a major
Students in A&S Business and Engineering must officially declare a major before junior year
notes: Could warn when completed credits approach 60 with no major selected

rule_summary: Only degree-seeking students may declare a minor; must have declared a primary major first
Minor should be declared by junior year for appropriate advising and completion
Courses in which a passing grade is earned may be repeated only once
The repeated course must be taken at Marquette
When a course is repeated the highest grade earned is included in cumulative GPA and the lowest grade excluded
Colloquiums co-ops independent study internships practicums seminars study abroad and variable title courses allow unlimited repeats
Courses with F NC WF U UNC or UY may be repeated until a passing grade is assigned; then one more repeat allowed
Transferred credit with grade below C only applies toward requirements that do not require C or better
Major-specific transfer courses taken more than 10 years before matriculation are not accepted; some colleges have shorter sunset
AP IB CLEP A-Level and PLTW credit awarded on official test scores not credits from prior institutions; may apply to MCC major minor or electives
Test credits revoked when student enrolls and earns credit in the course for which test credit was previously awarded
Students must obtain written approval for each course before enrolling at another institution; unapproved credits may be denied
Courses at 5000+ level cannot satisfy MCC requirements; undergrad filter restricts MCC buckets to 1000-4999
Transfer students placed into one of four MCC completion levels based on status at admission
notes: Would need transfer student status and MCC placement level

rule_summary: Normal CoBA undergraduate load is 12-19 credits per term; exceeding 19 requires overload form and dean approval

policy_name: CoBA C or better in major courses
rule_summary: CoBA students must earn C or better in each major requirement and elective including the core course introducing the major
notes: minimum_grade tag partially surfaces this; not enforced at allocation

rule_summary: Accounting students must earn C or better in ACCO 1030 and ACCO 1031 before enrolling in ACCO 3001
notes: Could enforce as hard prereq for ACCO 3001

rule_summary: At least one International Business course within or outside declared major is required for all CoBA students

policy_name: CoBA students cannot declare business minor
rule_summary: Undergraduate business students cannot declare a business minor; should pursue second business major instead
notes: server.py program_warnings; warning not hard block since non-CoBA students can minor in business

rule_summary: Undergraduate business students can complete a maximum of three business majors
Juniors/seniors may elect CR/NC for one course per term (max 4 total); only general electives eligible; not MCC BCC major or minor courses
notes: CR/NC grading not modeled in planner

rule_summary: Once matriculated at MU calculus and business courses must be taken at Marquette; business communication can be taken elsewhere with equivalency
Accelerated Degree Program 5000-level courses require 3.000 overall and 3.000 business GPA; max 2 classes per term
notes: Graduate program scope; not relevant to undergrad planner

rule_summary: Single-major CoBA students: upper-division business electives beyond major requirements can be below C if university and college GPA meets minimum
notes: Edge case; requires GPA and single-major context

rule_summary: C- D+ or D grades do not count toward major or minor credit hours but satisfy subject matter requirement and count toward 120 total; cognates can be completed at C-/D+/D
notes: No CAS programs in planner yet

rule_summary: CAS students exceeding 20 credits must submit Credit Overload Request form
Engineering students limited to 5 total uses of Course Repeat Option; same course max 3 attempts
Engineering students max 5 W grades and max 3 ADW/UW/WA/WF grades
Engineering students max 5 F or WF grades total (including replaced by repeat); max 3 F/WF per single course
Engineering courses expire after 8 years; expired courses must be retaken subject to repeat policy
CR/NC option may not be used for required courses or courses within student's specified major
Courses in same double-count family cannot be shared across buckets
Once a course in an NDC group is allocated the other group members are blocked from credit
Each additional bucket assignment must be pairwise-compatible with all already-assigned buckets
Cannot declare a minor in the same subject as a declared major
AIM BUAN and INBU majors require pairing with a primary major
B.A. in Arts & Sciences and International Business majors require second language proficiency

policy_name: Max courses per bucket per semester
rule_summary: Maximum 2 courses from any single bucket recommended per semester
When all non-elective buckets are full courses overflow into elective pools only
BCC_REQUIRED tier 1 before MCC_FOUNDATION tier 2 before late buckets tiers 3-5
Explorer style defers BCC when semesters remaining >= 4 and relax_bcc_band enabled
Bucket priority boosts from ranking_overrides.json clamped to max +-2
API accepts 1-15 recommendations per semester
API accepts 1-8 target semesters
Summer semesters exclude low_confidence courses from recommendations
